character.name:
A Vietnamese farmer from the countryside, standing in a rice field. The farmer has a tanned brown skin tone from working under the sun, a gentle and honest face, slightly wrinkled skin, high cheekbones typical of Southeast Asian people, dark brown eyes, short black hair slightly messy. The facial expression is calm, hardworking and friendly.

The farmer is wearing traditional Vietnamese rural clothing: a loose brown “áo bà ba” shirt made of simple cotton fabric. The shirt is long-sleeved, slightly wide and comfortable, with small round buttons in the front and two small pockets near the waist. The color is dark earthy brown, slightly faded from sun and farm work.

The pants are wide and loose, made of the same brown fabric, reaching down to the ankles. The fabric looks soft and slightly worn from daily farming work.

Around the neck is a traditional black-and-white checkered Vietnamese scarf called “khăn rằn”, made from thin cotton cloth. The scarf hangs loosely over the shoulders.

On the head is a traditional Vietnamese conical hat called “nón lá”. The hat has a wide circular brim and a pointed top, made from dried palm leaves woven together. The hat color is light beige with visible leaf texture and thin bamboo ribs underneath forming a radial pattern. A thin chin strap made from cloth hangs under the hat.

The farmer is holding a traditional farming hoe in one hand. The hoe has a long wooden handle and a dark metal blade slightly curved and worn from use.

The posture is relaxed but hardworking: standing upright with the hoe resting on the shoulder. The background shows green rice fields, muddy ground and water reflections typical of Vietnamese rice paddies. Warm natural sunlight, rural countryside atmosphere, realistic details.
